1989_09_07-India-Mumbai-We_Are_Never_Safe-In_Material_World-st2-vr03

Meta Data v7

Event Name/Title

We are never safe in material world

Event Date

07 Sep 1989

Event Location

India, Mumbai, Venue Name

Event Type

Academic

Transcriber

Alekhya Ippili

Completed

12 Sep 2021

Source Audio

 http://www.radhanathswamimedia.com/Lec/1989/89-043-We%20Are%20Never%20Safe%20In%20Material%20World%20(Mumbai)-OT-dt_89_09_07.mp3

Places

Mayapur,Vrindavan

Pastimes

Sringi’s curse on Maharaj Parikshit

Persons

Themes

e.g. Surrender, death, spiritual strength, courage,

Lecture Transcription

[Mangalacharan]

One is purified or unpurified or even having passed through all circumstances of life, one who remembers the beautiful personality of Godhead, whose eyes remem …..resemble the petals of the lotus flower, such a person is purified both within and without. Krishna describes in Bhagavad-gita, the most concise and elaborate system of knowledge that has ever been enunciated in the history of mankind. Krishna says in gita - Jnanam .tehan vishesata……...(inaudible...1.38-1.45)......... This means that - within this tree ties of Bhagavad gita I am explaining everything both phenomenal and nominal. We will know everything about this temporary relative world as well as about the supreme abode of the Lord. Bhagavad Gita explains how this material nature is working and why. Bhagwad gita describes the nature of the soul and its distinct existence apart from material creation. He describes how due to work for the desire of sense gratification, the spirit soul under illusion becomes further and further entangled in the complexities of karma. He describes how time, the all-devouring manifestation of the Lord in this creation is devouring, destroying everything, in every one; and he also describes His very own personal nature. How he is the source and the basis of the impersonal Brahman and how his divine personality is the supreme realization of the absolute truth.

janma karma ca me divyam

evam yo vetti tattvatah

tyaktva deham punar janma

naiti mam eti so’rjuna

[Bg 4.9]

[One who knows the transcendental nature of My appearance and activities does not, upon leaving the body, take his birth again in this material world, but attains My eternal abode, O Arjuna.]

How anyone who simply understands the spiritual nature of His appearance and activities in this world, such a person never takes birth in this material world again but attains the supreme abode of the Lord. And Krishna concludes Bhagavad-gita in the sixty six verse of the eighteenth chapter by declaring the very essence of all religious principles which is

sarva-dharmān parityajya
mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja
ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo
mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ

[Bg 18.66]

[Abandon all varieties of religion and just surrender unto Me. I shall deliver you from all sinful reactions. Do not fear.]

....that one should abandon all other philosophical and spiritual concepts and simply surrender to the Supreme Lord and how do we surrender to the Supreme Lord, that Krishna describes,

man-mana bhava mad-bhakto

mad-yaji mam namaskuru

mam evaisyasi sat yam

pratijane priyo si me

[Bg 18.65]

[Always think of Me, become My devotee, worship Me and offer your homage unto Me. Thus you will come to Me without fail. I promise you this because you are My very dear friend.]

We surrender to Krishna by always remembering Him. This is the very……. basic principle of all great religions of the world - to always remember the Supreme Lord of our life and to always execute his will at every step of our life. In this Srimad-bhagavatam, Sukadev-goswami repeats the same essential message to Maharaja Pariksit. Maharaja Pariksit was cursed to die by the son of the great brahman Samika rishi, whose name was Sringi. In seven days he cursed him that the very powerful mystic snake bird of the name Tataksa would bite him on the foot and cause his death. Maharaja Pariksit being a very sober and saintly person, rather than trying to avoid the inevitable,,, death, he welcomed the news and accepted that we are all about to die. Society is trying to make so many arrangements to prolong, the time delay between birth and death but the great souls, they try to purify and perfect their consciousness with whatever time is at their disposal. Sukadeva goswami told Maharaja Pariksit, that don't be attached to living a long life. The trees live thousands of years but what is the quality of their life, they are simply standing in ignorance. Sukadeva goswami told Maharaja Pariksit better to have one moment of consciousness of God than to live thousands and thousands of years without remembering Krishna. The duty of every human being and every human being is about to die sooner or later; the duty of every human being is to always remember Krishna; to always remember the supreme Lord of one's life. So Maharaja Pariksit sat for seven days and heard the narration of how great souls through-out history in the most prosperous conditions and in the most trying and testing circumstances, how they never forgot the Lord of their life. And then he described the personal glories of Lord Sri Krishna's pastimes in the tenth canto, which are so beautiful, so full of attraction for the souls, the very fulfilment of all our hearts desires, can be attained simply by hearing submissively the beautiful lila of the supreme Lord. And these pastimes, when they are heard from self-realized souls like Sukadeva goswami or those who are his representatives, they are fills full of philosophical truths which we can apply to our life and never be bewildered and never take one step off the path of pure devotion. And then in the twelfth canto of Bhagavatam, Srila Sukadev goswami, he could understand that the seven days had expired. That seven days that Maharaj Pariksit was sitting at his lotus feet had come to its end and now is the time when Maharaja Pariksit had to face the final examination of life, the moment of death. So Sukadev Goswami, was approached by Maharaja Pariksit and Maharaja Pariksit began to thank Sukadeva gosvami that it is my greatest fortune that this brahman Sringi has cursed me, because due to this condition of life, I have felt found shelter at your lotus feet and you out of your great kindness, your infinite unlimited inconceivable kindness, have been willing to sit here with me for seven days, ……… just to teach me how to perfect my life. Maharaja Pariksit was filled with thanks and gratitude and then Sukadev goswami gave his final instructions. He said everything you desire to hear from me you have heard. You desire to hear about the Supreme Lord Hari the source of everything, you desire to hear about the supreme goal of life, pure love for Him, these things have all been explained; now he said do not be afraid of this mystic snake bird Tataksa. You have nothing to fear, because the snake of death is going to destroy the body, no matter what you do, no matter what you say. I was listening to a tape by my guru Maharaj, his divine grace Srila Bhakti vedanta swami Prabhupada. And he was giving the example that death is so uncertain and so unavoidable that you just don't know when it is going to come. I knew one person, he was a friend of mine and he was telling me that, ‘’I have nothing to worry about because according to my horoscope which was done by that one of the greatest astrologers in all of India. I'm going to live till eighty two years old, so I can do anything I want, I have nothing to fear.’’ After all astrology is a bona fide Vedic science so it's true and a year and a half after he told me this he died. Death is very uncertain. Srila Prabhupad was giving this lecture in Mayapur. In Mayapur there was a four-story building and Srila Prabhupad said, ‘’That this morning we found a venomous serpent on the roof of our building’’. I said how is that possible, how in the world did a roof ....I’m sorry; how in the world did a snake get to the roof of our building. Prabhupad said, ‘’Snake means death’’. The serpent is the symbol of death in this world because at the least expected moment, just as a serpent can just come out of nowhere and strike, in the same way the serpent of death can strike at any moment. And you can make all arrangements to be sleeping on the roof and even then the serpent is there; how he got up there Prabhupad said ‘Nobody knows but he got up there.’’ So in this way Prabhupad said ‘’Krishna's trying to teach us that wherever you are, whatever condition of life you are in, you are never safe; safe - except if you are remembering Krishna because then you are beyond birth and death.’’ Sukadeva goswami said this snake bird Tataksa will inevitably come but he can only destroy your body which is composed of five material elements but he cannot touch you –the soul. Try to understand that you are the consciousness, you are the pure spiritual entity that is giving life to this body. And then he gave many wonderful examples to show the difference between the body and the soul and how the his soul is immune to any condition of life, if it is remembering Krishna. So the final instruction to Sukadeva goswami was simply fix your consciousness on Krishna and you have nothing to fear, there is nothing that can harm you.

And then Maharaja Pariksit hearing those words became full of courage, spiritual strength and then he told Maharaj; he with folded palms, he approached Sukadeva goswami and he said my dear guru Maharaj thank you again and again for your kindness. Now with your permission and with your blessings, I will face this snake bird Tataksa and with my mind fully absorbed in the beautiful thoughts of Krishna as I have heard from you, I will leave this world. And then Sukadeva goswami gave his full blessings and he departed [Applause] and as he left, everyone that was present there - all the great sages, the demigods who were watching from the heavens, they were feeling themselves most fortunate that they had witnessed the coming and the going of Sukadev Goswami on this holy spot on the banks of the Ganges. Sukadev Goswami disappeared into the forest and then Maharaj Pariksit taking shelter of the holy banks of mother Ganges, he sat on a mat of sacred and holy grass. I believe it was the darbha grass and each stem of the grass was facing the east. He sat with his back erect, he completely subdued all of his senses he gained full control of his life air and in this condition, in a state of utter fearlessness, he fixed the entirety of his consciousness on the lotus feet of Lord Hari, Sri Krishna. At that time sent by Sringi, the son of that great brahman, the Tataksa, the snake bird he knew now it was his time. The seven days had expired and he began to walk through the forest toward the bank of the Ganges, to offer death - a supreme gift that he had prepared for the great emperor Parikshit. As he was walking, he came upon Kashyapa Muni. He became very worried because of all the sages who were present, Kashyapa Muni was the only one who had the power to counteract the effects of this poison. In fact previously he had at one time, Tataksa with his fangs, he injected his venom into a palm tree and his poison was so powerful that that palm tree immediately was lit with fire and within seconds it burned to ashes and Kashyapa Muni came to that place and revived and restored the life of that palm tree. Tataksa was thinking Kashyapa muni is walking one way and I am walking the other way and he began to offer many many gifts to Kashyapa Muni just to pacify him and get him so he wouldn't disturb his mission. So Kashyapa Muni accepted those gifts and went on his way. And then the great snake bird Tataksa by his mystic power he took the form of a brahman, a very gentle loving brahman. Try to understand that in this world, there is many many deceiving elements. In fact the whole creation of Maya is one big orchestrated plan meant for your deception. Those things which appear to be the nicest resting place, where we can find shelter and happiness are usually the embodiments of death, huh ? Attachment to things of the senses means death. When you are not attached to anything you are beyond death. As soon as you become attached to the objects of the senses, you become attached to the body, which is the reservoir of your senses. And to the degree you're attached to the sense objects and to your body, to that degree, you have to accept death. But if you are completely detached then you are also detached from the body and then you have conquered death, because for the soul there is no death. Death is only for the body. So try to understand, all the objects of the senses which looks so pleasing and so wonderful and so trusting within this world, it is all the serpent of death in disguise; huh? do you understand ? So the serpent of death disguised himself as a brahman and he walked right up to maharaja Pariksit. And Maharaja Pariksit, he knew who had come and everyone around knew who had come and then that snake bird in the form of Brahman, he bent down and with his venomous fangs, he bit the foot of Maharaj Parikshit. At that time instantly flames began to glow and Maharaja Pariksit within seconds was consumed by this flaming fire of the poison of the great snake. And as he was being consumed, he was sitting in a perfectly sober and transcendental state of consciousness, simply remembering the beauty of the names and the glories of Sri Krishna. And within seconds he entered the supreme destination of life, the eternal abode of Sri Krishna. And at that time everyone was astonished, they were shocked how it actually happened. Although they were shocked and fascinated by this most remarkable form of departure, that took place right before their eyes, they all began to celebrate. The demigods began to play their kettle drums and all the great sages began to joyously celebrate because there was not a person there that did not have complete faith that Maharaja Pariksit had gone back home, back to Godhead. He had perfected his life. Therefore it was the time for joyous celebration. So try to understand there is nothing wrong with death. For a devotee of the Lord, death is glorious, it is simply his ticket back to Godhead. And for one who is not devoted to the Lord, death is nothing more than a change of clothes for some -

vasamsi jirnani yatha vihaya

navani grhnati naro parani

tatha sarirani vihaya jirnani

anyani samyati navani dehi

[Bg 2.22]

[As a person puts on new garments, giving up old ones, the soul similarly accepts new material bodies, giving up the old and useless ones.]

Krishna explains like this in the Bhagavad-gita. At that time the great son of Maharaja Pariksit, whose name was Janmajaya, he became very angry at this snake bird Tataksa that he has killed my father, I will take revenge. And he gathered together many, many great learned Brahmans and told them I want to perform a sacrifice and in this sacrifice I want all the snakes in the whole universe to be consumed by the fire of that sacrifice. So they lit the sacred fire and they began to chant the proper mantras and at that time all the serpents, the most powerful, to the most insignificant, they were all just dragged by the astral airwaves to just be flown into the fire and snakes just started coming down from all directions and they all started flying in to the fire and being utterly burned to ashes consumed. At that time Tataksa, he was very upset - I'm afraid although he was very expert at giving death, he was not very expert at accepting death. So he- he went to the planet of Indra and he prayed to Indra please protect me I am taking shelter of you and you alone. So Indra, he said yes you have come to me for shelter, I will give you shelter. Meanwhile the serpents were all being devoured by this fire. At that time the son of Maharaja Parikshit, he said to the brahmans why I have seen so many great powerful serpents coming entering to this fire to meet their death, I have not seen Tataksa yet. He's the one I want most where is he? The Brahmans they said unfortunately he has taken shelter of Indra. So Janmajaya said, “Then if Indra is so impudent to give him shelter, then let us chant a mantra so both him and Indra will be cast into the fire”. So the Brahman said, ”So be it”. They chanted the mantra that Indra and Tataksa enter into this fire and die. At that time Indra who is on his carrier, just lost all balance and both Indra and Tataksa were both flying toward the fire helplessly. At that time Brihaspati who was the guru of the demigods, he approached Janmajaya. He said listen you should not be so attached to killing Tataksa he's, he's after all he has tasted the nectar of the immortal, nectar of the demigods is not so easy to kill and Indra has a very responsible post, why are you so much angry at them you should know that whatever difficulties come in our life, nobody else is to blame it is all the reactions of our previous karma, we should not be revengeful toward anyone in this world. We should see all disturbances, all inconveniences as our own doing reactions of what we have done and not be envious of any living being. So the great son of Maharaja Parikshit, he stopped the sacrifice. Out of respect and love for this great spiritual master Brihaspati and in this way the Srimad-bhagavatam describes how the great Parikshit maharaja left this world. But Jiva Goswami and Vishwanath Chakravarti Thakur explain also that maharaja Pariksit was not bitten by this snake bird Tataksa due to his past sinful karma. A devotee is never subjected to ordinary material karma. One who takes shelter of the Lord and always remembers the Lord, he is always under the divine protection of Krishna. Krishna says in the seventh chapter of Gita –

daivi hy esa guna mayi

mama maya duratyaya

mam eva ye prapadyante

mayaam etam taranti te

[Bg 7.14]

[This divine energy of Mine, consisting of the three modes of material nature, is difficult to overcome. But those who have surrendered unto Me can easily cross beyond it.]

That those great souls who surrender to me, who takes shelter of me, they are no longer under the jurisdiction of this material energy. This material energy is very difficult to overcome, not only difficult it is impossible to overcome; but those who surrender to me they are no longer subjected to the ways of karma, they are under my personal protection, at all times. One who always remembers me, I always remember him. One who knows no one but me, I know no one but him. One who takes shelter of me by remembering me, by chanting my holy names, to that person he is always at my lotus feet. ....tatra ….................(inaudible 30.04).. ……. trishtati mad Bhakta ........... Krishna says wherever my devotees are chanting my holy names, I am there as the supreme protector, as the supreme Lord of their life. So Maharaj Parikshit, by the arrangement of Lord Sri Krishna, [Applause] [Music] he was put into a situation, where his pure love would be an example for all men and women to follow for the rest of time. How he met his death in full Krishna consciousness, how he lived his life and ended his life, in submissive devotion to the sadhus and in the divine remembrance of Lord Hari. So let us take example, from him. There is no greater treasure to acquire within our lives, than Krishna consciousness. All else we strive for, all else we possess, is but an illusion. It is here today, tomorrow it is gone. The only permanent asset we have in our life, is the remembrance of the Lord Hari and the service we offer him in that remembrance. Maharaj Parikshit could have done anything his last seven days but he chose to think of Krishna, to chant and hear his glories. So let us make our life a beautiful, a wonderful, meaningful life. A life that is no longer subject to fear or anxiety, a life that is constantly devoted to remembrance of the all beautiful, all merciful supreme personality of Godhead. By remembering Krishna we become purified from within and without [Applause] and for the pure soul there is only, [Applause] the mercy of God that he sees in every step of his life and therefore he is always peaceful. He is always in a state of love, of compassion, he is always tasting the ultimate beauty of life and that gift is available for you today, if you accept it. How to accept it, is very simple. By surrendering to Krishna, by chanting his name, through following the words of the great souls. That is all Maharaj Parikshit did, he simply heard Sukadev Goswami and followed his words and chanted the name of God, that same benediction is yours.Thank you very much. Hare Krishna. Is there any questions. [Applause]

Q&A Transcription

Never mind and especially ......... ................. (inaudible 34.12 - 34.56)................ [Applause] Kashyapa Muni was not an ordinary living being, understand that he was the father of Vamana dev, the supreme personality of Godhead. [Applause] You see Krishna's in the heart of every living being. Krishna wanted Maharaj Parikshit to go back home that day, back to Godhead. He did not want Kashyapa muni to interfere. [Applause] So therefore by the arrangement of Krishna, within the heart of Kashyapa muni, he accepted the gifts and went on his way. It wasn't like a materialistic man being paid off, he was directly under the supervision of Krishna. [Applause] If Kashyapa muni would have stayed maharaj Parikshit would not have died in seven days, huh ? Krishna wanted him to leave his body in seven days, he wanted to show the example of how a man should die. [Applause] [Music] My contention is not on that because ......... ................. (inaudible36.18 - 36.50) ..................... [Applause] You are not an ordinary soul, no ordinary soul could dance like you dance, its your inspiration, its Krishna name name ... [Applause] your message is very lovely, your company is enchanting …............................... (inaudible 37.12-37.16) ................…… I am your servant ….… and I am your servant,……… .................(inaudible 37.18-37.20)... together we are Krishna’s servants. Why is it that to get a PhD, you don't just pay your tuition money and they don't give you your diploma, why do you have to go through so much work. As you need to be educated before you're qualified after all you go to college, you pay your tuition. I know they, to just give you your degree why do you have to go oh my god about 12 years of school sometimes studying tests, so many troubles, so many tribulations you have to give up so many things you like to do huh? because you have to qualify yourself before you get that diploma and that takes sacrifice and at anytime you can drop out, correct ? there are a lot of tribulations, there's a lot of hardships and studying. So similarly why as soon as we become devotees, why don't we just become pure souls and go back to Godhead. For the same reason, because Krishna wants to see the quality of our love, he wants us to develop the quality of our love, then love is developed through the process of sacrifice and if we didn't have to undergo the tests of lust and anger and envy and pride and greed and illusion and all these horrible things that are within us, that we have created ourselves through all of our previous births, we have created the condition of our consciousness but Krishna wants us to face all of these testing elements within our life and with faith and conviction and determination and with trust in the guidance of the great souls and faith in His words, He wants us to pursue to work, to strive to sacrifice for him and when he sees that quality in us, then by his mercy, he very gracefully awards love of God to us. You see love of God is not something that you can acquire, it is already within you but you'll never find it on your own, it is only when Krishna reveals it by his own sweet will, that you can understand where it is and he reveals it when he sees that you have qualified yourself through sincere devotion and sacrifice through passing through so many tests; then you have showed your love for him, huh ? In the Brihad Bhagavatam written by Sanatan Goswami, there's a story of a lit of a spirit soul that just returns to Goloka Vrindavan and after being in this material world and Lord Sri Krishna sees the spirit soul, he has come and resumed his original form as a cowherd boy and Krishna's so glad to see him because he's been away from the spiritual world for countless millions of lifetimes; millions and millions and millions of years and all the cowherd boys in the pasture see that their friend has returned after a long time and Krishna when he sees him, he runs towards him and with his Lotus arms he embraces him and they are both embracing one another and tears are flowing from their eyes and Krishna expresses to him that I am so happy that you have returned to me. He says I know you I know what you went through for me, you had to conquer lust and envy and greed and anger and pride, things that are very difficult and painful to give up and you were persecuted and you were harassed for me for my sake [Applause] and you had to give up everything that was near and dear and precious to you for my sake, how much pain, how much sacrifice you offered for me, just to come back to my abode, how much I love you for this. In this way Krishna staged a beautiful festival of love to welcome his pure devotee back to the kingdom of God. We have a personal relationship with Krishna when we return back to Godhead. Don't think that Krishna doesn't know all that you had to go through to get there. He knows it all and how much that increases your appreciation for him for his mercy and how much he reciprocates by appreciating the gestures of the sacrifice of your love and that is what makes your re-entrance to Goloka vrindavan meaningful and beautiful, huh ? [Applause] or any other questions. [Applause]

The state of consciousness for some person if he is on the death bed, …...................(inaudible 43.20- 43.41)……..intelligently understands on the bed it is against me but he has not developed love for God …. is not dead of ...... …...................(inaudible)................. so what is the fate of such a person? Unless one is Krishna conscious no one is peaceful at the time of death; they may appears to be so to our eyes but the traumatic experience that the subtle body is undergoing at the time of death is a very horrifying experience, save and accept one, who sees that Krishna has come in the form of death in such a person with open arms embraces death with love, huh? [Applause] To the degree you are God-conscious at the time of death, to that degree [Applause] you advance towards the spiritual world. Such a person will take another birth, he will obviously, if he's a religious man he will take a pious birth- a religious birth and he will have the opportunity to continue on the path of religion until ultimately he surrenders to God. At the point of surrendering to God then he's beyond birth and death
